message:
docs: Update model download instructions and remove Git LFS

Models are no longer tracked in Git due to GitHub LFS 2GB file size limit.
Models range from 4.4GB to 13GB, exceeding this limit.

Changes:
- Remove .gitattributes (no longer using Git LFS)
- Update README.md with direct Hugging Face download instructions
- Rewrite MODELS.md with comprehensive download guide
- Add .gitignore entries for *.gguf files
- Remove models from git history via filter-branch

Users now download models directly from Hugging Face, which is:
- Standard practice for open-source ML projects
- No bandwidth limits
- Faster repository clones
- More flexible (any model, any quantization)

Model files remain available locally for development.

files.patch:
@@ -1,11 +0,0 @@
-# Git LFS configuration for Zell
-
-# Track model files with Git LFS
-*.gguf filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
-
-# Track LoRA adapter files
-*.lora filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
-
-# Track other large binary files
-*.bin filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
-*.safetensors filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text

@@ -1,10 +1,10 @@
 # Model Files
 
-This repository uses Git LFS (Large File Storage) to manage model files.
+Models are **not included** in this repository due to their large size (4GB-13GB per model). This document explains how to download and use models with Zell.
 
 ## Available Models
 
-### Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct (Q4_K_M)
+### Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct (Q4_K_M) - Recommended
 - **Size:** 4.4GB
 - **File:** `models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf`
 - **Description:** 7B parameter coding model, quantized to Q4_K_M for efficient inference
@@ -18,151 +18,161 @@ This repository uses Git LFS (Large File Storage) to manage model files.
 - **Best for:** Complex code generation, architectural decisions, larger context needs
 - **Context:** Varies by version
 
-## Setup Instructions
+## Downloading Models
 
-### First-time Setup
+### Option 1: Download from Hugging Face (Recommended)
 
-1. **Install Git LFS:**
-   ```bash
-   # macOS
-   brew install git-lfs
+```bash
+# Create models directory
+mkdir -p models
+
+# Download Qwen2.5-Coder-7B (recommended, 4.4GB)
+wget https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-GGUF/resolve/main/qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf \
+  -O models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf
 
-   # Ubuntu/Debian
-   sudo apt-get install git-lfs
+# Or download Codestral-22B (larger, 12GB)
+wget https://huggingface.co/bartowski/Codestral-22B-v0.1-GGUF/resolve/main/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf \
+  -O models/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf
+```
 
-   # Windows
-   # Download from https://git-lfs.github.com/
-   ```
+**Using curl instead of wget:**
+```bash
+# Qwen2.5-Coder-7B
+curl -L https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-GGUF/resolve/main/qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf \
+  -o models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf
 
-2. **Initialize Git LFS in your repository:**
-   ```bash
-   git lfs install
-   ```
+# Codestral-22B
+curl -L https://huggingface.co/bartowski/Codestral-22B-v0.1-GGUF/resolve/main/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf \
+  -o models/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf
+```
 
-3. **Clone the repository (this will automatically download LFS files):**
-   ```bash
-   git clone https://github.com/yourusername/zell.git
-   cd zell
-   ```
+### Option 2: Use Any GGUF Model
 
-### If You Already Cloned Without LFS
+Zell supports any GGUF-format model:
 
-If you cloned before setting up Git LFS, pull the actual model files:
+1. **Find models on Hugging Face:** Browse [GGUF models](https://huggingface.co/models?library=gguf)
+2. **Download to your system:** Any location works
+3. **Use with Zell:** Point to the model file path
 
 ```bash
-git lfs install
-git lfs pull
+# Build profile for any model
+zell build /path/to/your/model.gguf
+
+# Run inference
+zell run /path/to/your/model.gguf "Your prompt here"
 ```
 
-## Manual Download (Alternative)
+### Option 3: Convert Your Own Models
 
-If you prefer not to use Git LFS or hit bandwidth limits, download models manually:
+Use [llama.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp) to convert models to GGUF format:
 
-### Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct
 ```bash
-# Create models directory
-mkdir -p models
+# Clone llama.cpp
+git clone https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp
+cd llama.cpp
 
-# Download from Hugging Face
-wget -O models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf \
-  https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-GGUF/resolve/main/qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf
-```
+# Convert a Hugging Face model to GGUF
+python convert.py /path/to/huggingface/model --outfile model.gguf
 
-### Codestral-22B
-```bash
-# Download from Hugging Face
-wget -O models/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf \
-  https://huggingface.co/bartowski/Codestral-22B-v0.1-GGUF/resolve/main/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf
+# Quantize to Q4_K_M for efficiency
+./quantize model.gguf model-q4_k_m.gguf Q4_K_M
 ```
 
-## Usage
+## Quick Start After Download
 
-Once you have the models, build and run:
+Once you have a model:
 
 ```bash
-# Build optimization profile for the model
-./zig-out/bin/zell build models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf
-
-# Run inference
-./zig-out/bin/zell run models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf "Write a hello world in Zig"
+# 1. Build optimization profile (one-time, ~60 seconds)
+zell build models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf
 
-# With system prompt caching (faster subsequent runs!)
-./zig-out/bin/zell build models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf \
+# 2. Optional: Cache system prompt for instant startup
+zell build models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf \
   --system-prompt "You are a helpful Zig programming assistant."
+
+# 3. Run inference
+zell run models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf "Write hello world in Zig"
 ```
 
 ## Storage Requirements
 
-- **Git LFS Storage:** ~16.4GB total for both models
-- **Disk Space:** ~16.4GB + cache (~1-2GB for profiles and system prompts)
+- **Qwen2.5-Coder-7B:** ~4.4GB
+- **Codestral-22B:** ~12GB
+- **Cache files:** ~20-150MB per model (profiles, system prompts, conversation logs)
 
-**Note:** GitHub Free tier provides:
-- 1GB LFS storage
-- 1GB/month bandwidth
+**Total per model:** Model size + ~100MB for caches
 
-For repositories with these large models, consider:
-- **GitHub Pro** ($4/month) - 50GB storage, 50GB/month bandwidth
-- **External hosting** - Store models on Hugging Face and document download links
-- **Git LFS storage packs** - Purchase additional storage as needed
+## Model Sources
 
-## Adding New Models
+### Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct
+- **Hugging Face:** [Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-GGUF](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-GGUF)
+- **Original:** [Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct)
+- **License:** Apache 2.0
 
-To add a new model to the repository:
+### Codestral-22B
+- **Hugging Face:** [bartowski/Codestral-22B-v0.1-GGUF](https://huggingface.co/bartowski/Codestral-22B-v0.1-GGUF)
+- **Original:** [mistralai/Codestral-22B-v0.1](https://huggingface.co/mistralai/Codestral-22B-v0.1)
+- **License:** Check original model page
 
-```bash
-# 1. Place the .gguf file in the models/ directory
-cp /path/to/new-model.gguf models/
+## Performance Optimization
 
-# 2. Git LFS will automatically track it (configured in .gitattributes)
-git add models/new-model.gguf
+After downloading models, Zell automatically:
 
-# 3. Commit and push
-git commit -m "Add new model: new-model.gguf"
-git push
-```
+1. **Profile optimization** - Analyzes model and caches optimal GPU/CPU split
+2. **System prompt caching** - KV cache for 43.1% faster inference
+3. **Conversation logging** - Auto-learns from every interaction via LoRA
+4. **Metal shader compilation** - Pre-compiles GPU kernels (macOS)
 
-The `.gitattributes` file automatically tracks:
-- `*.gguf` - Model files
-- `*.lora` - LoRA adapter files
-- `*.bin` - Binary checkpoint files
-- `*.safetensors` - SafeTensors format files
+See [BENCHMARKS.md](BENCHMARKS.md) for detailed performance analysis.
 
 ## Troubleshooting
 
-### "This exceeds GitHub's file size limit"
-You need to use Git LFS. Follow the setup instructions above.
+### Download Failed or Interrupted
+
+```bash
+# wget supports resume
+wget -c https://huggingface.co/.../model.gguf -O models/model.gguf
+
+# curl supports resume
+curl -C - -L https://huggingface.co/.../model.gguf -o models/model.gguf
+```
 
-### "Git LFS bandwidth limit exceeded"
-Options:
-1. Wait for the monthly bandwidth reset
-2. Upgrade to GitHub Pro for more bandwidth
-3. Download models manually from Hugging Face
-4. Purchase additional LFS storage packs
+### Verify Download Integrity
 
-### Models not downloading during clone
 ```bash
-# Ensure Git LFS is installed
-git lfs install
+# Check file size (should match Hugging Face listing)
+ls -lh models/*.gguf
 
-# Pull LFS files manually
-git lfs pull
+# Qwen2.5-Coder-7B should be ~4.4GB
+# Codestral-22B should be ~12GB
 ```
 
-### Check LFS status
+### Model Not Working
+
 ```bash
-# See which files are tracked by LFS
-git lfs ls-files
+# Try rebuilding the profile
+zell build models/your-model.gguf --force
 
-# See LFS storage usage
-git lfs env
+# Check if it's a valid GGUF file
+file models/your-model.gguf
+# Should output: "models/your-model.gguf: data"
 ```
 
-## Performance Tips
+### Out of Memory
 
-After downloading models:
-1. Build the model once: `zell build <model.gguf>`
-2. Use `--system-prompt` during build for faster subsequent runs
-3. Profiles are cached in `~/.cache/zell/profiles/`
-4. System prompt caches are in `~/.cache/zell/system-prompts/`
+If you run out of memory:
+1. Use a smaller quantization (Q4_K_M is good balance)
+2. Use a smaller model (7B instead of 22B)
+3. Close other applications
+4. See [README.md](README.md) for memory requirements
 
-See [BENCHMARKS.md](BENCHMARKS.md) for detailed performance analysis.
+## Why No Git LFS?
+
+GitHub's Git LFS has a 2GB per-file limit, but our models range from 4.4GB to 13GB. Instead:
+
+- **Direct downloads** from Hugging Face (the canonical source)
+- **No bandwidth limits** for users
+- **Faster clones** since git doesn't fetch large files
+- **More flexible** - use any model, any quantization
+
+This is the standard approach for open-source ML projects.

@@ -250,12 +248,39 @@ try logger.markConsolidated(&[_]u64{ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 });
 
 ## Models
 
-Zell supports any GGUF-format model. Included models:
+Zell supports any GGUF-format model. Due to their large size, models are not included in the repository.
+
+### Recommended Models
+
+- **Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M** (4.4GB) - Recommended for coding tasks
+- **Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M** (12GB) - Larger, more capable for complex tasks
+
+### Downloading Models
+
+**Option 1: Download from Hugging Face**
+
+```bash
+# Create models directory
+mkdir -p models
+
+# Download Qwen2.5-Coder-7B (recommended)
+wget https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-GGUF/resolve/main/qwen2.5-coder-7b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf \
+  -O models/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf
+
+# Or download Codestral-22B (larger)
+wget https://huggingface.co/bartowski/Codestral-22B-v0.1-GGUF/resolve/main/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf \
+  -O models/Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M.gguf
+```
 
-- **Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M** (4.4GB) - Recommended for coding
-- **Codestral-22B-v0.1-Q4_K_M** (12GB) - Larger, more capable
+**Option 2: Use any GGUF model**
 
-Models are tracked via Git LFS. See [MODELS.md](MODELS.md) for setup instructions.
+Zell works with any GGUF-format model. Download from [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/models?library=gguf) or convert your own using [llama.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp).
+
+```bash
+# Use any model file
+zell build /path/to/your/model.gguf
+zell run /path/to/your/model.gguf "Your prompt here"
+```
 
 ---
